decisions.decision: Build the success-path breadth coverage on the existing `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` helper surface instead of adding new HTTP scaffolding, reusing `create_market_with_headers(...)`, `probability_edit_with_headers(...)`, `comment_post_with_headers(...)`, `event_trade_with_headers(...)`, `market_resolution_with_headers(...)`, and the shared `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` helper that already anchors the quota-header contract.
alternatives_rejected: Add route-specific request plumbing for each protected POST path instead of using the helper methods already present in the class.
Assert only ad hoc header presence on the new routes and bypass the shared quota-header helper.

decisions.decision: Keep ProbabilityEdit as the canonical surface for repeated quota math, `429`, `Retry-After`, and per-agent isolation, while the new create-market, comment-post, event-trade, and resolve success checks pin one accepted request per route to the full shared header contract with exact `limit` and `remaining` values.
alternatives_rejected: Clone the multi-request ProbabilityEdit limiter matrix onto every protected POST route in this step.
Reduce the new non-ProbabilityEdit checks to presence-only assertions and leave quota values unspecified.

decisions.decision: Treat `test_market_resolve_http_requires_valid_agent_id_when_enabled` as the stale resolve seam because its successful branch currently leaves rate limiting disabled and asserts `assert_rate_limit_headers_absent(valid_headers)`. Preserve the missing-agent, blank-agent, malformed-agent, and disabled-limiter no-header proofs, but move limiter-enabled resolve success coverage to a header-present assertion instead of keeping the stale expectation.
alternatives_rejected: Keep the enabled-limiter resolve success path on a no-header expectation.
Replace the existing resolve auth test wholesale and lose the disabled-limiter no-header coverage.

decisions.decision: Give comment-post first-class coverage with a dedicated success-path test that asserts both the accepted `comment` payload and the shared rate-limit headers, while create-market and event-trade participate as explicit named cases in the shared all-routes `subTest(...)` matrix because their missing seam is auth/rate-limit breadth rather than route semantics.
alternatives_rejected: Cover comment-post only as a loop participant without any route-specific payload assertion.
Add standalone route-specific success tests for create-market and event-trade even though they already have ordinary success coverage elsewhere in the file.

steps.task: Use the audited `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` helper set in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` as the implementation seam, specifically the existing `*_with_headers(...)` helpers for create-market, probability-edit, comment-post, event-trade, and market-resolve plus `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` and `assert_rate_limit_headers_absent(...)`.

Add one shared success-path breadth test in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` that turns on a positive `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` and exercises create-market, probability-edit, market-resolve, comment-post, and event-trade through explicit `subTest(...)` labels, asserting `201` plus `assert_rate_limit_headers(...)` with exact `limit` and `remaining` values after one accepted request per route.

Add a dedicated comment-post success test in the same class that asserts the accepted `comment` response payload together with the shared rate-limit headers so comment-post ends the step with first-class route-specific auth/rate-limit coverage.

Refactor the resolve coverage to remove the stale enabled-limiter no-header expectation from the successful resolve branch while preserving the current missing-agent, blank-agent, malformed-agent, and disabled-limiter no-header seams.

Run the focused `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` slice that covers the new success-path header assertions, inspect the resulting diff to confirm the change stays tests-only, and commit the worktree with the requested message.
